---
title: "DVD/CD-R tray won't eject"
date: 2008-08-28
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by DapperMe17 on 2008-08-28
Ubuntu Hardy

After loading Ubuntu, my DVD/CD-R will not play, or eject, without a "sudo umount" from the terminal.

This first occured after I changed sda5 & sda6 to External1 & External2 (from /disk), "sudo mkdir..." & "sudo CHOWN -R..." 

What do I need to do to solve this???


***My fstab...***

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/hda3 :
UUID=b3f86e43-239d-485d-9f72-735bfa55cec2 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda1 :
UUID=0000649A00001B58 /media/hda1 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_US.UTF-8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda4 :
UUID=4633-DE83 /media/hda4 vfat defaults,utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 1
# Entry for /dev/hda2 :
UUID=ad10c4c1-f699-493d-aa8f-09faef6f26bc none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hdc /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,auto,exec 0 0
/dev/sda5 /media/External1 ntfs-3g defaults 0 0
/dev/sda6 /media/External2 ntfs-3g defaults 0 0

---

### Post by arch0njw on 2008-08-29
CD drives are usually something like /dev/scd0.  For example, here is the entry from my fstab:

```
/dev/scd0       /media/cdrom0   udf,iso9660 user,noauto     0       0
```

---
